---
title: "Presidents × Legislation × Events"
subtitle: "Force-directed graph linking administrations to the laws they signed and the events that hit during their terms"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
---

```{=html}
<style>
  .net-container {
    display: flex;
    flex-direction: column;
    gap: 12px;
    width: 100%;
  }
  .net-controls {
    display: flex;
    flex-wrap: wrap;
    gap: 16px;
    align-items: center;
    padding: 10px 12px;
    background: #f8f9fa;
    border: 1px solid #dee2e6;
    border-radius: 6px;
    font-size: 0.9rem;
  }
  .net-controls label {
    display: inline-flex;
    align-items: center;
    gap: 5px;
    margin: 0;
    cursor: pointer;
  }
  .net-legend {
    display: flex;
    flex-wrap: wrap;
    gap: 24px;
    padding: 10px 12px;
    background: #fff;
    border: 1px solid #dee2e6;
    border-radius: 6px;
    font-size: 0.88rem;
  }
  .net-legend-item { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; }
  .net-color-scale {
    display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; margin-left: auto;
  }
  .net-color-scale .scale-bar {
    width: 180px; height: 12px; border-radius: 3px;
    background: linear-gradient(to right, #d62828, #f2c14e, #2f9e44);
    border: 1px solid #adb5bd;
  }
  #net-viz {
    width: 100%;
    height: min(80vh, 780px);
    min-height: 500px;
    border: 1px solid #dee2e6;
    border-radius: 6px;
    background: #ffffff;
    overflow: hidden;
  }
  #net-viz svg { width: 100%; height: 100%; display: block; cursor: grab; }
  #net-viz svg:active { cursor: grabbing; }
  .net-tooltip {
    position: absolute;
    pointer-events: none;
    background: rgba(29, 53, 87, 0.96);
    color: #fff;
    padding: 8px 10px;
    border-radius: 4px;
    font-size: 0.82rem;
    line-height: 1.3;
    max-width: 320px;
    box-shadow: 0 3px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.2);
    opacity: 0;
    transition: opacity 0.12s ease;
    z-index: 1000;
  }
  .net-tooltip b { color: #ffe5a0; }
  @media (max-width: 768px) {
    #net-viz { height: 520px; }
    .net-legend { gap: 12px; font-size: 0.8rem; }
    .net-color-scale { margin-left: 0; }
  }
</style>
```

Each **grey circle** is a presidential administration. Each **square** is a
piece of legislation; each **triangle** is a world event. **Colour** goes
red → amber → green along sentiment (−1 extreme negative → 0 neutral → +1
extreme positive), with hue interpolated in HCL space so the mid-point
reads as a clean amber rather than a muddy brown.

**Links:** solid grey lines connect presidents to events that happened
during their term and to legislation they signed. Dashed red lines show
presidents who publicly opposed legislation from other terms; solid green
lines show cross-term support. Line thickness tracks link strength.

Drag a node to pin it; scroll/pinch to zoom; hover for details.

::: {.callout-important appearance="simple"}
**Sentiment values are editorial first-pass estimates** anchored on
contemporaneous polling and NBER/FBI/public indicators. They are stored in
plain CSVs under `data/sentiment/` — `legislation.csv` and `events.csv` —
and are intended to be refined as we swap in derived sentiment from
GDELT/Media Cloud and issue-specific polling. See the
[Methodology](about.qmd) page for full caveats.
:::

```{=html}
<div class="net-container">
  <div class="net-controls">
    <strong>Show:</strong>
    <label><input type="checkbox" id="net-chk-pres" checked> Presidents</label>
    <label><input type="checkbox" id="net-chk-leg"  checked> Legislation</label>
    <label><input type="checkbox" id="net-chk-evt"  checked> Events</label>
    <label><input type="checkbox" id="net-chk-cross" checked> Cross-term support/opposition</label>
    <button type="button" id="net-reset" style="margin-left:auto; padding:3px 10px;">Reset layout</button>
  </div>
  <div class="net-legend">
    <span class="net-legend-item">
      <svg width="18" height="18"><circle cx="9" cy="9" r="7" fill="#6c757d" stroke="#333"/></svg>
      President
    </span>
    <span class="net-legend-item">
      <svg width="18" height="18"><rect x="2" y="2" width="14" height="14" fill="#bc4749" stroke="#333"/></svg>
      Legislation
    </span>
    <span class="net-legend-item">
      <svg width="18" height="18"><polygon points="9,2 16,15 2,15" fill="#2f9e44" stroke="#333"/></svg>
      Event
    </span>
    <span class="net-color-scale">
      <span>−1</span>
      <span class="scale-bar" title="Red = negative, Amber = neutral, Green = positive"></span>
      <span>+1</span>
    </span>
  </div>
  <div id="net-viz"></div>
</div>
<div id="net-tooltip" class="net-tooltip"></div>
<script src="../js/d3.v7.min.js"></script>
<script>
(function () {
  const container = document.getElementById('net-viz');
  const tooltip   = document.getElementById('net-tooltip');
  const width  = container.clientWidth || 1000;
  const height = container.clientHeight || 700;

  const svg = d3.select('#net-viz').append('svg')
    .attr('viewBox', [0, 0, width, height])
    .attr('preserveAspectRatio', 'xMidYMid meet');

  const rootG = svg.append('g');

  svg.call(d3.zoom()
    .scaleExtent([0.3, 4])
    .on('zoom', (ev) => rootG.attr('transform', ev.transform)));

  // HCL-interpolated sentiment scale (red → amber → green)
  const sentimentColor = d3.scaleLinear()
    .domain([-1, 0, 1])
    .range(['#d62828', '#f2c14e', '#2f9e44'])
    .interpolate(d3.interpolateHcl);

  function nodeFill(d) {
    if (d.type === 'president') return '#6c757d';
    if (d.sentiment == null || Number.isNaN(d.sentiment)) return '#adb5bd';
    return sentimentColor(+d.sentiment);
  }

  // Short president labels for on-node text
  const shortPresident = {
    'Bill Clinton': 'Clinton',
    'George W. Bush': 'Bush (43)',
    'Barack Obama': 'Obama',
    'Donald Trump (1st term)': 'Trump I',
    'Joe Biden': 'Biden',
    'Donald Trump (2nd term)': 'Trump II',
  };

  d3.json('../data/sentiment/network.json').then(graph => {
    // Deep-copy links for d3-force (it mutates the array)
    const nodes = graph.nodes.map(d => ({ ...d }));
    const links = graph.links.map(d => ({ ...d }));

    const sim = d3.forceSimulation(nodes)
      .force('link', d3.forceLink(links).id(d => d.id)
        .distance(d => d.role === 'in-office' ? 90 : 130)
        .strength(d => d.role === 'in-office' ? 0.7 : 0.35))
      .force('charge', d3.forceManyBody()
        .strength(d => d.type === 'president' ? -900 : -260))
      .force('center', d3.forceCenter(width / 2, height / 2))
      .force('collide', d3.forceCollide(d => d.type === 'president' ? 30 : 18));

    const linkG = rootG.append('g').attr('class', 'links');
    const nodeG = rootG.append('g').attr('class', 'nodes');

    const linkSel = linkG.selectAll('line')
      .data(links).enter().append('line')
      .attr('stroke', d => d.role === 'opposed'  ? '#bc4749'
                         : d.role === 'supported' ? '#2f9e44'
                         : '#adb5bd')
      .attr('stroke-opacity', 0.55)
      .attr('stroke-width', d => d.role === 'signed' ? 1.8
                              : d.role === 'in-office' ? 1.1 : 1.3)
      .attr('stroke-dasharray', d => d.role === 'opposed' ? '4,3'
                                   : d.role === 'supported' ? '2,2' : null);

    const nodeSel = nodeG.selectAll('g.node')
      .data(nodes).enter().append('g')
      .attr('class', 'node')
      .call(drag(sim));

    // Shape per type
    nodeSel.filter(d => d.type === 'president')
      .append('circle')
      .attr('r', 18).attr('fill', '#6c757d')
      .attr('stroke', '#1d3557').attr('stroke-width', 2);

    nodeSel.filter(d => d.type === 'legislation')
      .append('rect')
      .attr('width', 18).attr('height', 18)
      .attr('x', -9).attr('y', -9)
      .attr('fill', nodeFill)
      .attr('stroke', '#333').attr('stroke-width', 1);

    nodeSel.filter(d => d.type === 'event')
      .append('polygon')
      .attr('points', '0,-12 11,8 -11,8')
      .attr('fill', nodeFill)
      .attr('stroke', '#333').attr('stroke-width', 1);

    // President labels
    nodeSel.filter(d => d.type === 'president')
      .append('text')
      .text(d => shortPresident[d.label] || d.label)
      .attr('text-anchor', 'middle')
      .attr('dy', 32)
      .attr('font-size', 12)
      .attr('font-weight', 600)
      .attr('fill', '#1d3557')
      .attr('pointer-events', 'none');

    // Tooltip handlers
    nodeSel.on('mousemove', (ev, d) => {
      const rect = container.getBoundingClientRect();
      const parts = [`<b>${d.label}</b>`];
      if (d.type === 'president') {
        parts.push(`Party: ${d.party}`);
        if (d.meta) parts.push(d.meta);
      } else {
        parts.push(`Type: ${d.type === 'legislation' ? 'Legislation' : 'Event'}`);
        if (d.date) parts.push(`Date: ${d.date}`);
        if (d.sentiment != null) parts.push(`Sentiment: ${(+d.sentiment).toFixed(2)}`);
        if (d.meta) parts.push(d.meta);
      }
      tooltip.innerHTML = parts.join('<br>');
      tooltip.style.left  = (ev.pageX + 12) + 'px';
      tooltip.style.top   = (ev.pageY + 12) + 'px';
      tooltip.style.opacity = 1;
    }).on('mouseleave', () => {
      tooltip.style.opacity = 0;
    });

    sim.on('tick', () => {
      linkSel
        .attr('x1', d => d.source.x).attr('y1', d => d.source.y)
        .attr('x2', d => d.target.x).attr('y2', d => d.target.y);
      nodeSel.attr('transform', d => `translate(${d.x},${d.y})`);
    });

    function drag(simulation) {
      return d3.drag()
        .on('start', (ev, d) => {
          if (!ev.active) simulation.alphaTarget(0.3).restart();
          d.fx = d.x; d.fy = d.y;
        })
        .on('drag', (ev, d) => {
          d.fx = ev.x; d.fy = ev.y;
        })
        .on('end', (ev, d) => {
          if (!ev.active) simulation.alphaTarget(0);
          // leave fx/fy set so the node stays pinned; dbl-click to unpin
        });
    }

    nodeSel.on('dblclick', (ev, d) => {
      d.fx = null; d.fy = null;
      sim.alpha(0.4).restart();
    });

    // Filters
    function applyFilters() {
      const showPres  = document.getElementById('net-chk-pres').checked;
      const showLeg   = document.getElementById('net-chk-leg').checked;
      const showEvt   = document.getElementById('net-chk-evt').checked;
      const showCross = document.getElementById('net-chk-cross').checked;

      const typeVisible = t =>
        (t === 'president'   && showPres) ||
        (t === 'legislation' && showLeg)  ||
        (t === 'event'       && showEvt);

      nodeSel.style('display', d => typeVisible(d.type) ? null : 'none');

      linkSel.style('display', d => {
        const sv = typeVisible(d.source.type);
        const tv = typeVisible(d.target.type);
        if (!sv || !tv) return 'none';
        if ((d.role === 'supported' || d.role === 'opposed') && !showCross) return 'none';
        return null;
      });
    }
    ['pres', 'leg', 'evt', 'cross'].forEach(k =>
      document.getElementById('net-chk-' + k).addEventListener('change', applyFilters));

    document.getElementById('net-reset').addEventListener('click', () => {
      nodes.forEach(n => { n.fx = null; n.fy = null; });
      sim.alpha(1).restart();
    });
  }).catch(err => {
    container.innerHTML = '<p style="padding:20px;color:#bc4749;">Failed to load network data: ' +
                          (err && err.message ? err.message : err) + '</p>';
  });
})();
</script>
```

## Reading the graph

- **Cluster shape** — presidents and their in-term items form radial
  lobes. Events and legislation sit at ~equal distance from their
  president node because `forceLink` distances are equal across
  in-term links.
- **Cross-term edges** (dashed red / dotted green) pull certain
  legislation between two presidents, shortening the distance to the
  supporter/opposer and visually connecting administrations that took
  positions on someone else's law.
- **Drag a president** to pin it; the rest of the graph reorganizes
  around that fixed point. Double-click a node to release the pin.

## Data sources

Stored in two CSVs under `data/sentiment/`:

- **`legislation.csv`** — hand-curated list of major 1999-to-present US
  federal legislation with contemporaneous sentiment estimates, the
  president who signed, and (optional) cross-term `supported_by` /
  `opposed_by` keys.
- **`events.csv`** — world and domestic events with a sentiment estimate
  and severity tag. Already used by the Economic and Media Sentiment
  pages for event-line overlays.

The graph is assembled by `R/build_network.R` and emitted as
`data/sentiment/network.json`, consumed by D3 on this page. Re-running
the build is cheap: no external API calls, just CSV → JSON.

## Where sentiment comes from (and should come from)

Current sentiment values are **editorial** — a single author's reading of
contemporaneous polling, NBER/FBI indicators, and published retrospective
commentary. The intention is that the numbers evolve as we plug in derived
signals:

- **GDELT tone** around the signing date (already cached on the
  [Media Sentiment page](media.qmd)) gives a media-framing signal per
  legislation.
- **Media Cloud volume** lets us weight the signal by how much coverage
  each law received.
- **Issue-specific polling** (Pew, Gallup issue tracking) would ground the
  approval estimate.

Until then, the CSVs are plain-text and sit under version control — each
score has a `notes` field explaining the anchor. PRs welcome.
